app.title: Interactive Cosmic Explorer
app.description: Explore the universe in interactive 3D — solar system, stellar neighborhood, constellations, and beyond.

library.title: Library
library.subtitle: Browse celestial objects, fun facts, and your saved bookmarks

empty.bookmarks: You haven't saved any bookmarks yet. Save camera views from the Explorer to see them here.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: An error occurred while rendering the cosmic explorer. Your progress has been saved.

notFound.title: Lost in space
notFound.description: The cosmic coordinate you followed does not lead anywhere in our universe.

header.title: Cosmic Explorer
header.subtitle: Explore planetary orbits in real time

bookmarks.title: Bookmarks
bookmarks.empty: No bookmarks yet. Press Ctrl+B to save your current view.

settings.title: Settings
settings.highQualityDesc: Full particle count, high resolution. Best for powerful devices.
settings.lowQualityDesc: Reduced particle count, capped resolution. Best for low-end devices.

bookmarksLoadFailed.title: Couldn't load bookmarks
bookmarksLoadFailed.description: Your saved views will appear once the connection is restored.

progressLoadFailed.title: Couldn't load progress
progressLoadFailed.description: Your exploration stats will resume once we reconnect.

progressTrackFailed.title: Couldn't save progress
progressTrackFailed.description: Your latest action may not be saved.

achievementsLoadFailed.title: Couldn't load achievements
achievementsLoadFailed.description: Your badges will appear once the connection is restored.

library.title: Library
library.description: An encyclopedia of planets, stars, and constellations is on its way.

help.title: Help
help.faq1: How do I navigate the 3D scene?
help.faq1Answer: Use mouse drag to rotate, scroll to zoom. Press ? for keyboard shortcuts.
help.faq2: How do I save my current view?
help.faq2Answer: Press Ctrl+B (or Cmd+B on Mac) to open the bookmark modal.
help.faq3Answer: Explore planets, stars, and constellations. Each interaction contributes to your progress.

layout.subtitle: Sign in to continue exploring planets, orbits, space missions, and cosmic quizzes.

login.title: Login to Cosmic Explorer
login.subtitle: Continue your journey through the solar system
login.signupSuccess: Account created successfully! Please log in.

signup.title: Sign up to Cosmic Explorer
signup.subtitle: Start your journey exploring the solar system
signup.checkEmail: Check your email to confirm your account

welcome.title: Welcome to Cosmic Explorer
welcome.description: An interactive journey through our solar system and beyond. Let's explore the universe together.

sun.title: The Sun
sun.description: Our star at the center of the solar system, containing 99.8% of all the mass in the solar system.

earth.title: Earth
earth.description: Our home planet — the only known world harboring life, with liquid water oceans and a protective atmosphere.

saturn.title: Saturn
saturn.description: The jewel of the solar system, famous for its spectacular ring system made of ice and rock particles.

neptune.title: Neptune
neptune.description: The farthest planet from the Sun, 30 times Earth's distance away, with winds reaching 2,100 km/h.

stellar.title: Stellar Neighborhood
stellar.description: Zoom out beyond our solar system. Thousands of nearby stars become visible within 100 light-years.

galactic.title: The Milky Way
galactic.description: Our home galaxy — a spiral galaxy containing 200 to 400 billion stars, 100,000 light-years across.

cosmic.title: The Observable Universe
cosmic.description: The largest scale — the entire observable universe, 93 billion light-years in diameter, containing trillions of galaxies.

finish.title: You're Ready to Explore!
finish.description: You've seen the full scale of the cosmos. Now explore freely — click planets, zoom in and out, discover the universe.
